title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. paramushirensis
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. paramushirensis, also known as Paramushir bentgrass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Paramushir Island in the Kuril Islands of Russia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. paramushirensis can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
